1.week1: Inspect stored paddy for moisture — keep below 14% to prevent fungal growth.
1.week2: Clean and service the thresher before post-harvest processing.
1.week3: Apply mulch to harvested fields to conserve soil moisture for the next crop.
1.week4: Collect soil samples for testing — results guide your Kharif fertiliser plan.
alerts: Watch for rodent damage in grain storage
Low temperature can affect germination of nursery beds

2.week1: Deep plough the field to expose soil-borne pests to winter cold.
2.week2: Apply Farm Yard Manure (FYM) at 10 tonnes/ha and incorporate into soil.
2.week3: Check bunds and levelling — uneven fields waste 30% more irrigation water.
2.week4: Order certified seed for summer nursery if planning a June transplant.
alerts: Soil temperature still low — delay nursery sowing until March

3.week1: Prepare raised nursery beds (1m wide, 10m long) with well-drained soil.
3.week2: Treat seeds with Carbendazim 2g/kg to prevent seed-borne diseases.
3.week3: Sow pre-germinated seeds at 40g/m² for optimal seedling density.
3.week4: Apply light irrigation twice daily — avoid water stagnation in nursery.
alerts: Monitor for blast disease in nursery — apply Tricyclazole if spotted
Aphid infestation risk rising with warming temperatures

4.week1: Apply urea top-dressing at 5g/m² to nursery beds for vigorous seedling growth.
4.week2: Weed nursery beds manually — do not use herbicides at this stage.
4.week3: Thin out overcrowded seedlings to improve air circulation.
4.week4: Seedlings should be 20–25 days old (3–4 leaves) before transplanting.
alerts: Leaf folder moths active — use light traps to monitor population

5.week1: Transplant seedlings at 2–3 per hill, 20cm × 15cm spacing for Kharif varieties.
5.week2: Apply basal fertiliser: DAP 50kg/ha + MOP 40kg/ha before transplanting.
5.week3: Maintain 2–5cm standing water for the first 2 weeks after transplanting.
5.week4: Gap-fill any missing hills within 7 days of transplanting.
alerts: Stem borer active — apply Chlorpyrifos granules at transplanting
Check weather forecast — avoid transplanting just before heavy rain

6.week1: Apply first nitrogen split (urea 65 kg/ha) at 15 days after transplanting (DAT).
6.week3: Drain field for 3–4 days at tillering stage to strengthen root system.
6.week4: Scout for brown plant hopper (BPH) — check base of plants weekly.
alerts: Monsoon onset — risk of blast increases significantly
Zinc deficiency (yellowing leaves) — apply ZnSO4 at 25 kg/ha

7.week1: Apply second nitrogen split (urea 65 kg/ha) at 45 DAT.
7.week2: Maintain 5–8cm water level during active tillering.
7.week3: Pull out and destroy any Tungro-infected plants immediately.
7.week4: Install pheromone traps for stem borer monitoring (1 trap/ha).
alerts: High humidity — ideal conditions for Sheath Blight. Apply Validamycin if threshold exceeded
BPH outbreak risk peak month

8.week1: Apply potash top-dressing (MOP 20 kg/ha) to improve grain quality.
8.week2: Apply third urea split (urea 30 kg/ha) at panicle initiation stage.
8.week3: Ensure no water stress during panicle initiation — critical stage.
8.week4: Scout for false smut balls on panicles — remove and burn affected ones.
alerts: Neck blast is peak risk now — spray Tricyclazole 0.6g/L at boot stage
Avoid broad-spectrum pesticides to protect natural enemies of BPH

9.week1: Maintain 3–5cm water during flowering — drought stress reduces yield by 40%.
9.week2: Do NOT spray pesticides during flowering — protects pollinators.
9.week3: Scout for grain discolouration — sign of bacterial blight or false smut.
9.week4: Begin field drainage 10 days before expected harvest for efficient combine operation.
alerts: Cyclone watch — lash paddy bundles if storm predicted
Grain damage from sparrows rises at dough stage — use scare devices

10.week1: Harvest when 80–85% of grains are golden yellow — avoid over-maturity.
10.week2: Use combine harvester or self-help group thresher to reduce losses.
10.week3: Dry harvested paddy to 14% moisture before storage.
10.week4: Register at nearest paddy procurement centre before closing date.
alerts: Unseasonal rain — cover harvested crop with tarpaulin immediately
Price advisory: MSP for common paddy is ₹2,183/quintal (2025–26)

11.week1: Chop and incorporate straw residue — avoids burning and improves soil health.
11.week2: Apply Trichoderma viride to decompose stubble faster.
11.week3: Irrigate field lightly and sow green manure (Dhaincha) as rabi break crop.
11.week4: Clean and store all farm equipment before the dry season.
alerts: Stubble burning ban in force — violations attract ₹2,500 fine
Apply lime if soil pH < 5.5

12.week1: Apply gypsum (500 kg/ha) on alkaline soils for soil amendment.
12.week2: Renew Kisan Credit Card before expiry for next season credit.
12.week3: Attend local KVK (Krishi Vigyan Kendra) training sessions if available.
12.week4: Plan next Kharif crop — select variety based on Bloomsense disease heatmap.
alerts: Fog and low temperatures — protect nursery beds with polythene covers

1.week1: Apply first irrigation (CRI stage) if 20–25 days have passed since sowing.
1.week2: Apply second urea top-dressing (65 kg/ha) at first irrigation.
1.week3: Scout for yellow rust on upper leaves — apply Propiconazole if spotted.
1.week4: Check for aphid colonies at leaf tips — threshold is 10 aphids/tiller.
alerts: Yellow rust spore movement is high in January
Low temperature may delay tillering in late sown crops

2.week1: Apply third irrigation at tillering stage (40–45 DAS).
2.week2: Spray 2,4-D sodium salt for broad-leaf weed control.
2.week3: Apply second nitrogen split at tillering.
2.week4: Monitor for Karnal Bunt in areas with high humidity.
alerts: Aphid outbreak risk peaks in February
Frost warning — delay irrigation if frost predicted in next 48 hours

3.week1: Apply fourth irrigation at jointing stage.
3.week2: Scout for stem rust — warm and humid nights increase risk.
3.week3: Apply foliar micronutrients (Zinc + Boron) for grain quality.
3.week4: Remove volunteer plants of different varieties.
alerts: Hailstorm risk in North India — assess crop insurance coverage

4.week1: Apply fifth irrigation at booting stage — most critical irrigation.
4.week2: Do NOT apply nitrogen at this stage — will increase lodging risk.
4.week3: Monitor for aphids on ear heads — spray imidacloprid at threshold.
4.week4: Check for powdery mildew on upper leaves.
alerts: Unseasonal rain + high humidity — Scab (Fusarium head blight) risk elevated

5.week1: Apply sixth (final) irrigation at grain filling if available.
5.week2: Avoid any chemical spray — residue risk in grain.
5.week3: Estimate yield and contact local mandi for registration.
5.week4: Harvest when grains are fully dry and hard (< 12% moisture).
alerts: Hot dry winds (loo) accelerate maturity — may reduce grain weight

6.week3: Sell or store in jute bags.
alerts: MSP for wheat is ₹2,275/quintal (2025–26)
Soil moisture conservation critical in July

10.week4: Sow if soil temperature is below 25°C.

11.week1: Sow wheat in first fortnight of November for timely sown crop.
11.week2: Seed rate: 100 kg/ha for timely sown, 125 kg/ha for late sown.
11.week3: Apply basal dose: Urea 65kg + DAP 100kg + MOP 40kg per ha.
11.week4: First irrigation (pre-sowing) if soil moisture is low.
alerts: Delay in monsoon withdrawal may push sowing into late window — use late-sown varieties like PBW 373

12.week1: Ensure uniform germination — resow if < 70% germination.
12.week2: First irrigation at Crown Root Initiation (CRI) — 20–22 DAS.
12.week3: Scout for termite damage — apply Chlorpyrifos if needed.
12.week4: Remove weeds manually before first irrigation.
alerts: Fog may reduce sunlight — monitor for elongated, weak seedlings

1.week4: Apply Ca+Mg foliar to prevent BER.
alerts: Spider mites active in dry cold weather

2.week1: Avoid excess nitrogen at flowering stage.
2.week2: Install sticky traps for whitefly monitoring.
2.week3: Scout for Tomato Leaf Curl Virus (TLCV) — remove infected plants.
2.week4: Apply potassium foliar for fruit set.
alerts: TLCV spread by whitefly peaks in Feb-March

3.week3: Scout for fruit borer — apply biopesticide (HaNPV).
alerts: Price dips in March — consider cold-chain or processing options

4.week1: Pull out crop residue and destroy.
4.week2: Prepare nursery beds for Kharif tomato.
4.week4: Apply shade net on nursery to protect from heat.

5.week4: Apply mulch film to conserve moisture.
alerts: High rain — waterlogging is fatal. Ensure raised beds
Monsoon humidity — bacterial speck risk high
Late blight can destroy crop in 7 days — act immediately
Thrips and jassids — early action critical

7.week4: Apply growth regulator (Mepiquat Chloride) if vigorous.
alerts: Pink bollworm — most damaging pest of Bt cotton in India

9.week3: Apply foliar potash for boll opening.
9.week4: Sell at nearest CCI procurement centre.
alerts: MSP for medium staple cotton: ₹7,121/quintal (2025–26)

10.week2: Apply defoliant if late bolls not opening.
10.week3: Pull out stalks after last pick.
10.week4: Destroy stalks — reduces next season pest carryover.

alerts: Fall Armyworm — check whorls daily

7.week2: Scout for Fall Armyworm in whorls.
7.week3: Apply Emamectin Benzoate for FAW control.
alerts: FAW is the #1 threat to Kharif maize — inspect whorls every 3 days

8.week2: Detassel male plants in seed production plots.
8.week3: Ensure no water stress at silking.

9.week4: Harvest when husk is fully dry.

10.week4: Sell or store with hermetic bags.
alerts: MSP for Maize: ₹2,090/quintal (2025–26)

11.week1: Sow Rabi maize in irrigated conditions.
